produces.artifact: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/decision-log.md (structured decision-log format guide, auto-generated per fr-sd-015)
scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/pending-conflict.md (pending-conflict handoff format guide, auto-generated per fr-sd-015)
scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js (programmatic validators + facade re-exports of decision-log / pending-conflict rules)
scripts/lib/sdd-rules.js (canonical home for DECISION_LOG_RULES and PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES constants; split from sdd-utils.js to keep file size below 700-line hard limit)

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Location and Filesystem-Determined Shape
requirement.description: The design-doc schema MUST define (a) where design docs live, (b) how their identity (spec-id, iteration) is derived from the path, and (c) which conditional sections apply based on filesystem state. Per the "no modes" decision in design.md §2, the schema is single-behavior — a filesystem check drives the expected conditional sections; there is no mode selector. The schema rules live in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js as the exported DESIGN_DOC_RULES constant (see fr-sd-010).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify design doc location as docs/plans/<spec-id>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/design.md, with spec-id derived from directory name (kebab-case) and iteration date derived from date directory name. REQ-F003

The schema MUST specify that filesystem state determines which conditional sections the refiner expects: specs/<spec-id>/spec.xml exists → expect Context + Change Intent; does not exist → expect prose with problem/solution/requirements/scope. This is a single-behavior filesystem check, not a mode selector. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

Section heading detection MUST tolerate trailing suffix text on the heading line (e.g., "## Context (from spec v1)", "## Context — 2026-04-19") while rejecting prefix words like "## Contextual Factors". The regex form is anchored with a word boundary after the keyword and accepts any non-newline content to end-of-line (equivalent to /^#+\s+Context\b[^\n]*$/im for Context, same shape for Change Intent). The explicit pattern lives in DESIGN_DOC_RULES.section_regex. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Structure When No Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the minimum content requirements for a design doc produced when no prior spec exists for the target spec-id.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify minimum content for this case as: problem description or motivation, proposed solution or architecture, identifiable requirements (in prose), and scope declaration (includes/excludes). No fixed section headings required — the refiner extracts from prose. REQ-F003
The schema MUST include a valid example showing a complete design doc with all minimum content elements identifiable. REQ-F003
The schema MUST include an invalid example: an empty or stub design doc (e.g., only "TODO: fill in later"), with error message: "design doc has no substantive content". REQ-F008

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Structure When Prior Spec Exists
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the required sections for a design doc produced when a prior spec exists for the target spec-id.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify two required sections: Context (current spec state summary, 2-3 sentences, spec version reference) and Change Intent (what is changing and why). Missing either section is ERROR. REQ-F005
The schema MUST specify one recommended section: Architecture Impact (how changes interact with existing design). Optional for simple changes, recommended for cross-cutting changes. REQ-F005
The schema MUST include a valid example with Context, Change Intent, and Architecture Impact sections. REQ-F005

The schema MUST include invalid examples: (1) missing Context section with error "iteration design doc missing required Context section", (2) missing Change Intent section with error "iteration design doc missing required Change Intent section". REQ-F005, REQ-F008

The schema MUST forbid pre-authored structured delta sections (no explicit ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED lists in the design doc). The LLM refiner derives the delta from narrative intent; the design doc carries only human-authored narrative. REQ-F005

requirement.title: Design Doc Schema — Validation Summary
requirement.description: The schema MUST include a validation summary table showing all checks, their type (deterministic vs LLM judgment), and applicability per path.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The validation summary MUST list these deterministic checks: file existence (ERROR if missing, always), substantive content (ERROR if empty/stub, always), Context section heading present (ERROR if missing, when prior spec exists), Change Intent section heading present (ERROR if missing, when prior spec exists), stale iteration date (WARNING if date <= spec's design_iteration, when prior spec exists). REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F026

The validation summary MUST list these LLM judgment checks: requirements identifiable in prose (ERROR if none found, when no prior spec exists), scope declared (ERROR if missing, when no prior spec exists). REQ-F003

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Required Fields
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/spec.md MUST define all required fields in the spec.xml identity header (<overview> element).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST define these always-required fields with types: spec_id (string, kebab-case, must match folder name), spec_version (positive integer, starts at 1), status (enum: "active"), title (string, human-readable), description (string, brief scope summary), source/design_path (string, valid file path to design doc), source/design_iteration (date, YYYY-MM-DD). Missing any field is ERROR. REQ-F010
The schema MUST define the conditionally-required supersedes field: required when spec_version > 1, format "<spec-id>:v<N>". Missing supersedes for v2+ is ERROR. REQ-F010

The schema MUST define the scope element: includes (list of features with id attribute and text description, always required) and excludes (list of reasons, recommended). Empty includes is WARNING. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Detail File Structure
requirement.description: The schema MUST define the structure of detail files referenced from spec.xml.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST specify that spec.xml references detail files via <detail_file path="details/<name>.xml" /> elements, and each detail file contains <requirement> elements. REQ-F012

The schema MUST specify each <requirement> element requires: id attribute (unique, e.g., "fr-bs-001"), <title>, <description>, and <acceptance_criteria> with at least one <criterion>. Each criterion MUST have an id attribute and a <trace> element referencing the source requirement. REQ-F012

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header Schema — Examples
requirement.description: The schema MUST include valid and invalid examples for LLM pattern-matching.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The schema MUST include a valid v1 spec example: complete <overview> with all required fields, no supersedes, scope with includes and excludes. REQ-F010
The schema MUST include a valid v2+ spec example: complete <overview> with supersedes set to "<spec-id>:v1", updated source pointing to new design iteration. REQ-F010

The schema MUST include invalid examples with error messages: (1) missing spec_version, (2) broken design_path pointing to non-existent file, (3) missing supersedes for v2+, (4) empty scope as WARNING. REQ-F010, REQ-F008

requirement.title: SDD Utils — Design Doc Validator API
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export functions for parsing and validating design docs. These implement the deterministic checks from fr-sd-004.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MUST export parseDesignDoc(filePath: string) that returns {spec_id, iteration, hasPriorSpec, hasContext, hasChangeIntent, hasSubstantiveContent, specDesignIteration} derived from the file path and content. Returns null if file not found. spec_id and iteration are extracted from the directory path, not from file content. hasPriorSpec is a plain filesystem fact (does specs/<spec_id>/spec.xml exist?) — it is NOT a "mode" and the return type MUST NOT expose a "mode" field, per design.md §2 and fr-bs-002. REQ-F003, REQ-F005
MUST export validateDesignDoc(parsed) that returns {valid: boolean, issues: Array<{level: "ERROR"|"WARNING"|"INFO", field: string, message: string}>}. valid is true when zero ERRORs. REQ-F008

validateDesignDoc deterministic checks: file exists (ERROR), content not empty/stub (ERROR), when prior spec exists: Context heading present (ERROR), Change Intent heading present (ERROR), iteration date not stale vs spec's design_iteration (WARNING). REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F026

requirement.title: SDD Utils — Spec Header Validator API
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export functions for parsing and validating spec.xml identity headers.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MUST export parseSpecHeader(specXmlContent: string) that returns {spec_id, spec_version, status, design_path, design_iteration, scope, supersedes?} or null if header not found. Parses the <overview> element from XML content. REQ-F010
MUST export validateSpecHeader(parsed) that returns {valid: boolean, issues: Array<{level, field, message}>}. valid is true when zero ERRORs. REQ-F010

validateSpecHeader deterministic checks: all required fields present (ERROR per missing field), spec_version is positive integer (ERROR), design_path points to existing file (ERROR), design_iteration matches YYYY-MM-DD format (ERROR), supersedes present when spec_version > 1 (ERROR), supersedes format matches "<spec-id>:v<N>" (ERROR), empty scope includes (WARNING). REQ-F010

requirement.title: Schema Rules Are Exported Constants (Single SoT)
requirement.description:
Design-doc and spec-header schema rules MUST live as exported constants in scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js (DESIGN_DOC_RULES and SPEC_HEADER_RULES). The newer DECISION_LOG_RULES and PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES constants (added in v2 per fr-sd-012, fr-sd-013) are DEFINED in a sibling module scripts/lib/sdd-rules.js — split from sdd-utils.js to keep the latter under the 700-line hard limit per coding-standards — and RE-EXPORTED through scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js so consumers can import all four rule constants from a single facade. Both module paths are valid import sources for the re-exported pair; the canonical home (definition site) is sdd-rules.js.
Validators (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader, parseDecisionLog, validateDecisionLog, parseConflictMarker) MUST consume these constants — not duplicate the rules as regex literals inside the function bodies. Downstream consumers (skills, tests, the print-schema CLI) MUST read from these exports rather than redescribing the schema in hand-authored text.
This is the structural defense against the drift that produced the 2026-04-19 "Context heading" bug: the skill said one thing, the schema doc said a second, and the validator regex enforced a third. With rules centralized, validators and documentation are guaranteed to agree by construction.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen DESIGN_DOC_RULES object containing: canonical_path, path_regex, substantive_min_chars, section_regex (with Context and ChangeIntent entries), iteration.required_sections, iteration.recommended_sections, iteration.forbidden_section_keywords, initial.required_prose_elements. REQ-F003, REQ-F005

scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen SPEC_HEADER_RULES object containing: canonical_path, design_iteration_regex, supersedes_regex, required_fields, conditional_fields, scope, delta (with placement, ordering, last_delta_invariants, and child_element_rules). REQ-F010

Validator functions (parseDesignDoc, validateDesignDoc, parseSpecHeader, validateSpecHeader) MUST reference the exported rule constants for regexes and thresholds. Duplicating a rule as a local regex literal inside a function body is ERROR and constitutes drift risk. REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F010

requirement.title: _pending-conflict.md Schema Definition
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ MUST include a schema describing the _pending-conflict.md handoff format (per fr-cc-if-007), and scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export the corresponding rule constants (PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES, frozen). This makes the handoff format validator-addressable by Phase 6 mechanical authorization check (per fr-rf-010-ac5) and by brainstorming Phase 0 detection (per fr-bs-008).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES object containing: canonical_path ("specs/<spec-id>/_pending-conflict.md"), required_fields (axis_fired, conflict_description with cited design line ranges and Q&A q_ids, candidate_resolutions list with len(1..=3), user_action_prompt), lifecycle (ephemeral, deleted by brainstorming on new-design write). 2026-04-27:B.2, 2026-04-27:B.3

The schema MUST forbid persistent or versioned variants — the file is non-authoritative ephemeral by design (per A.1's narrowed Iron Law). Validators MUST treat any attempt to persist the file across a completed conflict cycle as ERROR ("a prior conflict cycle did not complete cleanly"). 2026-04-27:A.1, 2026-04-27:B.3

The schema MUST include both a valid example (axis 1 fired, 2 candidate resolutions, user-action prompt present) and an invalid example (no candidate resolutions) with error message: "_pending-conflict.md MUST contain at least one candidate resolution". 2026-04-27:B.2

requirement.title: Structured Decision-Log Schema Definition
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/ MUST include a schema describing the structured decision-log format (per fr-cc-if-008), and scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export the corresponding rule constants (DECISION_LOG_RULES, frozen). This makes the decision-log format validator-addressable by refiner's Phase 4 axis 2 check and Phase 6 mechanical authorization check.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export a frozen DECISION_LOG_RULES object containing: canonical_path (relative to brainstorming's output directory), required_fields_per_row (q_id, question, user_answer_verbatim, deferral_signal), q_id_uniqueness (per-session unique), deferral_signal_canonical_phrases (at minimum: "use defaults", "covered.", "skip", "you decide"). 2026-04-27:B.1

The schema MUST specify that rows are addressable by q_id — lookup by q_id is a deterministic operation on the parsed structure (no LLM re-interpretation needed for trace verification). 2026-04-27:B.1, 2026-04-27:B.4

The schema MUST include a valid example (3 Q&A rows with distinct q_ids, mix of deferral_signal=true and false) and invalid examples: (1) duplicate q_id within a session, (2) missing deferral_signal field, (3) missing user_answer_verbatim field. 2026-04-27:B.1

requirement.title: SDD Utils — Conflict and Decision-Log Validator API
requirement.description: scripts/lib/sdd-utils.js MUST export functions for parsing and validating the _pending-conflict.md handoff and the structured decision-log, plus a Phase 6 mechanical authorization helper. These implement the deterministic checks needed by refiner per fr-rf-010-ac5 and by brainstorming per fr-bs-008.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MUST export parseConflictMarker(filePath: string) that returns {axis_fired, conflict_description, candidate_resolutions, user_action_prompt} or null if file not found. The function MUST validate against PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES (per fr-sd-012) and surface schema violations as parse errors. 2026-04-27:B.2, 2026-04-27:B.3

MUST export parseDecisionLog(filePath: string) that returns an array of row objects, each containing {q_id, question, user_answer_verbatim, deferral_signal}, plus validateDecisionLog(parsed) that returns {valid, issues} per the same shape as validateDesignDoc / validateSpecHeader (per fr-sd-008-ac2 / fr-sd-009-ac2). Validation MUST enforce DECISION_LOG_RULES (per fr-sd-013). 2026-04-27:B.1, 2026-04-27:B.4

MUST export a Phase 6 mechanical authorization helper that, given an in-memory spec XML, the design.md file path, and the decision-log file path (when present), iterates over every <trace> element and verifies the cited content appears at the cited source location: design line ranges checked against the design file content, Q&A q_id refs checked against the decision-log row's user_answer_verbatim. Returns {valid, unauthorized_traces} where unauthorized_traces lists every trace whose cited content is missing. 2026-04-27:B.4

Validator functions MUST reference the exported rule constants (PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES, DECISION_LOG_RULES) for their schema invariants — not duplicate the rules as local literals inside function bodies. Drift between rules and validators is ERROR per fr-sd-010-ac3's pattern (single source of truth). 2026-04-27:B.1, 2026-04-27:B.2

requirement.title: Schema Access Pattern — Direct Read Primary, CLI Retained
requirement.description:
Skills and agents that need to know an SDD artifact's schema have two equivalent access paths: (a) direct read of the markdown view at ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/<name>.md, and (b) invoking the CLI via node ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/scripts/lib/print-schema.js <name>. Both paths emit the same content because the markdown view is auto-generated from the same rule constants the CLI reads (per fr-sd-010, drift-protected per fr-sd-016). Direct read is the recommended primary form in skill prose because it avoids the Bash + Node + stdout indirection and works uniformly across agent platforms; the CLI remains a valid alternative and is the canonical tool for contributor regeneration of the markdown view.
This requirement exists because hand-authored schema copies in skills produced the 2026-04-19 "Context heading" bug. The auto-generation pipeline (fr-sd-015 + fr-sd-016) makes drift structurally impossible across all four schemas (design, spec, decision-log, pending-conflict).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/print-schema.js MUST exist and accept four targets (design, spec, decision-log, pending-conflict) plus --markdown and --json flags. Default output is human-readable text. --markdown emits the committed-markdown form (with the AUTO-GENERATED header). --json emits the raw rule object with RegExp values encoded as <regex>pattern</regex> strings. Unknown targets MUST exit non-zero with a usage message. REQ-F003, REQ-F010

print-schema.js MUST derive its output from the exported rule constants (DESIGN_DOC_RULES, SPEC_HEADER_RULES, DECISION_LOG_RULES, PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES) imported via the sdd-utils.js facade (which re-exports the latter two from sdd-rules.js per fr-sd-010). It MUST NOT hand-author schema content. Prose narrative inside renderer functions (lines.push patterns) is permitted as renderer-template content; structured fields MUST come from the rule constants. REQ-F003, REQ-F010

arc-brainstorming, arc-refining, and any other SDD-pipeline skill SHOULD reference the schema via direct read at ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/<name>.md as the primary form in skill prose. Direct read MUST use the ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/ prefix per cc-005 — bare scripts/lib/ paths in skill prose are forbidden. The CLI form node ${ARCFORGE_ROOT}/scripts/lib/print-schema.js <name> is an equivalent valid alternative and MAY appear in skill prose where it fits (e.g., when the skill needs a specific flag like --json). SDD skills MUST NOT embed structural code-block templates that duplicate schema content. REQ-F003, REQ-F005, REQ-F010

requirement.title: print-schema.js Renderer Extension for Decision-Log and Pending-Conflict
requirement.description: scripts/lib/print-schema.js MUST grow two renderer functions (renderDecisionLog and renderPendingConflict) following the same pattern as renderSpec and renderDesign. Each renderer pulls structured field data from its rule constant (DECISION_LOG_RULES or PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES) and emits prose narrative via lines.push() string literals — the renderer function body is the natural template location for prose, just as the existing renderers do for spec.md and design.md. The two new markdown files at scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/decision-log.md and scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/pending-conflict.md MUST be regenerated via the CLI (--markdown) and carry the existing AUTO-GENERATED header used by spec.md and design.md.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/print-schema.js MUST export renderDecisionLog(opts) and renderPendingConflict(opts) functions matching the existing renderSpec/renderDesign signature (markdown flag in opts, returns joined string). The functions MUST be wired into main()'s target dispatch so that `node print-schema.js decision-log` and `node print-schema.js pending-conflict` produce output. design.md:Schema axis section

Each renderer MUST source structured fields (canonical_path, required_fields, deferral_signal_canonical_phrases, lifecycle, etc.) from the corresponding rule constant — DECISION_LOG_RULES for decision-log, PENDING_CONFLICT_RULES for pending-conflict. Prose narrative (canonical path explanation, lifecycle discussion, examples) MUST live in the renderer function body as lines.push('...') string literals, NOT as a "narrative" field inside the rule constant. design.md:Schema axis section

scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/decision-log.md and pending-conflict.md MUST be regenerated via `node print-schema.js decision-log --markdown > scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/decision-log.md` (and equivalent for pending-conflict) and MUST carry the existing AUTO-GENERATED header (the same comment block used by spec.md and design.md). After regeneration, hand-edits to these files are forbidden — modifications go to the rule constant or the renderer function body. design.md:Schema axis section

requirement.title: Drift Gate Extension to All Four Schemas
requirement.description: tests/scripts/sdd-schemas-fresh.test.js MUST be extended to cover all four sdd-schemas/*.md files (design, spec, decision-log, pending-conflict). The test asserts byte-equal between the committed markdown file and the renderer output, blocking CI on any drift. This is the structural enforcement of fr-sd-011's "two equivalent access paths" claim — without this gate, hand-edits to the markdown could silently diverge from the rule constants that validators consume.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: tests/scripts/sdd-schemas-fresh.test.js MUST contain four per-schema test cases (one each for design.md, spec.md, decision-log.md, pending-conflict.md). Each case MUST read the committed file, invoke the corresponding renderer (with markdown flag), and assert byte-equal after trailing-whitespace normalization. design.md:Schema axis section

On test failure, the diff hint output MUST identify the first differing line with both committed and generated content (matching the existing diffHint helper pattern). Remediation guidance MUST point to the regeneration command: `node scripts/lib/print-schema.js <name> --markdown > scripts/lib/sdd-schemas/<name>.md`. design.md:Schema axis section
